messages.content: You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What is a variable in Python?
A variable is a name that points to a value in memory. Example: `count = 3` stores 3 and lets you reuse `count` later.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What does `def` do?
`def` defines a function. Example: `def greet(name): return f"Hello, {name}"` creates a reusable block of code.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
Why use a virtual environment?
A virtual environment isolates project dependencies so different projects can use different package versions without conflicts.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What is overfitting?
Overfitting means the model memorizes training examples instead of learning general patterns, so it performs poorly on new data.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What is a learning rate?
Learning rate controls how big each weight update is during training. Too high can be unstable; too low trains very slowly.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What is fine-tuning?
Fine-tuning starts from a pretrained model and continues training on your dataset so the model adapts to your task or style.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
What is a token?
A token is a chunk of text the model reads or writes. One word can be one or several tokens depending on the tokenizer.
You are a friendly coding tutor. Keep answers short and practical.
How do I avoid leaking secrets into git?
Store secrets in `.env`, add `.env` to `.gitignore`, and use environment variables in code instead of hardcoding keys.
